Commentary: Brad Evans [3.0-0-21-0] is back into the attack
Zimbabwe have been penalised for slow over-rate. There will be five fielders inside the ring for the final over. Bangladesh need 5 off the last 6 balls.
Brad Evans to Tanzid Hasan Tamim, 1 run, a low full-toss angling across Tanzid. He reaches out and slices it to deep backward point
Brad Evans to Mosaddek Hossain, THATS OUT!! Bowled!!

Brad Evans to Mosaddek Hossain, out Bowled!! Knocked him over! The crowd has come alive! A low full-toss angling in on off, Mosaddek clears his front leg and tries to smash it through covers but gets beaten on the under-edge and the ball crashes into the base of the off-stump. He would be gutted with himself for missing a straight one. This game is not over yet. Mosaddek Hossain b Brad Evans 1(3)
Mohammad Saifuddin, left handed bat, comes to the crease
Brad Evans to Saifuddin, THATS OUT!! Caught!!

Brad Evans to Saifuddin, out Caught by Ryan Burl!! Two in two for Evans! Bangladesh are making life difficult for themselves! Fuller length delivery shaping across Saifuddin. He shuffles across and mistimes his expansive shot over wide mid-on. Burl runs in from the deep and takes it easily. This is turning out to be a nail-biter. Saifuddin c Ryan Burl b Brad Evans 0(1)
Mahedi Hasan, right handed bat, comes to the crease

Oh, Evans had probably trapped Mahedi in front but the umpire calls it a dead ball. What drama! It was a low full-toss that shaped back in, Mahedi tried to hit it across but missed to get pinged on the pads. It looked adjacent to the naked eye. There was a fielder moving in the outfield and hence, the umpire had called it a dead ball just before Evans released it. Evans and the rest of the Zimbabwe players walk up towards the umpire. Raza has an animated discussion with the umpire. Tanzid and Evans have a heated exchange. Tempers flaring up

Brad Evans to Mahedi Hasan, FOUR, the game is done and dusted! So much drama at the end but Bangladesh have prevailed! A full-toss on off, Mahedi Hasan makes some room and hits it over extra cover for a boundary. Tanzid celebrates animatedly and has something to say to Evans. That stirs Evans up and there's another heated exchange between the two. Marumani steps in and tries to calm his mate down. What a game! Bangladesh win the series 2-1

16:11 Local Time, 14:11 GMT, 19:41 IST: Evans threatened to win the game from the jaws of defeat for his side in the final over but Mahedi kept his cool to take his side over the line. Bangladesh made life difficult for themselves but they have managed to pull it off. Apart from Bennett and Myers' good contributions, no other Zimbabwean batter made it into double-figures. Myers' 73 helped the hosts to get to a fighting total but it was always going to be below-par even on a slightly variable surface. I reckon they were at least 15-20 runs short. In response, the duo of Ngarava and Muzarabani bowled brilliantly with the new ball and kept the Bangladeshi top-order on their toes. Tanzid and Emon hit a few boundaries in quick time after the powerplay and that eased the pressure on them. Emon couldn't capitalise on his start but the 55-run stand between the duo kept the visitors ahead in the game.

Tanzid then found an able ally in Hridoy and the duo kept the scoreboard ticking and also scored the odd-boundary. Hridoy fell against the run of play but he had done his job. The 63-run partnership between them had put Bangladesh in the driver's seat. Yasir Ali fell in the penultimate over but the visitors had things well under control. They needed 5 off the last over - Evans took two in two to turn it into a nail-biting contest. Evans had probably trapped Mahedi in front and it would have been a hat-trick for him but much to Zimbabwe's dismay, the umpire had already called it a dead ball before Evans delivered the ball (a fielder moving in the outfield being the reason for the dead ball). Mahedi held his nerves and hit one over covers to send his side into a frenzy...

Raza (2/2): (On taking on India in four days' time and the positives from this series) I thought some of the positives have been the few partnerships that we have got. But the main real positive has been our bowling lineup on these Bulawayo surfaces, how well we have bowled, especially the seamers. The spinners have contributed here and there, but ideally, in my opinion, the biggest contribution in this series has been from the seamers, and I think that's a big positive. (On becoming the most-capped cricketer for Zimbabwe across formats today) It was made more special by the little presentation that Zimbabwe Cricket and Hammy (Hamilton Masakadza) did. But it was made even more special that my son was here. He wasn't supposed to be here. He was with his school, and he asked me to pick him up. I didn't know that there was going to be a ceremony and things like that. To have my son with me here suddenly made it even more special.

Tanzid Hasan Tamim | Player of the Match and the Series: First of all, Alhamdulillah, we won the series. It's an amazing feeling. I think our bowlers did a really good job. (On his approach with the bat in this series) Yeah, I think not to play too much hard, just watch the ball and play the merit of the ball. That's the main thing. (On the feelings in the dressing room) Yeah, we're feeling amazing because we lost the last two series in the Tests and ODIs, so winning the last match is a really amazing feeling. I hope the boys are enjoying it also. (On being stranded at the non-striker's end in the final over) Yeah, no problem. I was trying to keep myself normal. I think if I end up not losing my wicket, then I can easily finish the match. That's what I believe in that particular situation.

Towhid Hridoy: (Happy with the performance of his lads?) Alhamdulillah, so far we did well as a batting unit and bowling unit. The last two matches we dominated and we controlled the game. (On their comeback after losing the first T20I) When we lost the first match, then we had a meeting and we discussed the conditions and how we would go forward. Our boys really played well. As a bowling unit and batting unit, they really played well. (On Rishad Hossain) We know about Rishad, his ability. When he gets the opportunity, he shows his character, and he's dominated with the ball here. (On Tanzid) Yeah, he's a class batsman. We know his abilities, and whenever he gets in, he always tries to finish the game, and he shows that for the team.
So, here's the glory moment! Towhid Hridoy has been presented the trophy and he is all smiles! He poses for photographs with his mates.

That's it from our side! Bangladesh end the tour on a high with a T20I series win after disappointing performances in the one-off Test and ODI series. Zimbabwe, though, showed plenty of fight and continue to take encouraging strides as a team. We hope you enjoyed our coverage. Until next time, goodbye and cheers!
